original_abstract: This article examines the pressing need to reform organizational and management mechanisms within higher education institutions to better meet the demands of the modern socio-economic landscape. The author highlights the transition from centralized control toward institutional autonomy, focusing on the implementation of academic and financial independence as catalysts for healthy competition and innovation. By analyzing various international management models—including liberal and centrist approaches—the study contextualizes Uzbekistan’s current educational reforms, such as the adoption of credit-module systems and the enhancement of public-private partnerships. Central to the discussion is the role of management culture, leadership ethics, and the integration of both socio-psychological and rational-economic factors in boosting institutional efficiency. The findings emphasize that modernizing management principles is essential for producing competitive, highly qualified specialists capable of contributing to national development and achieving international recognition in global university rankings.
humanized_abstract: The article reveals the necessity of reforming the organizational and management structures in the higher education system to bring them in line with the new socio-economic conditions. According to the author, the vector of development is shifting from the centralized state management to the institutional autonomy and academic and financial independence of universities which will entail rivalry and innovations. Based on the analysis of the liberal and centrist approaches to university management and foreign models of governance, the author links them to the reforms being implemented in Uzbekistan in today’s era of education reform, which includes the introduction of a credit-module system and increasing the role of public-private partnership. A special place is given to the management culture, the ethical standards of leadership, and a combination of socio-psychological and rational-economic components of the efficiency of the higher education institution. As the author believes, the modernization of the management activities in higher education institutions is vital to training an innovatively-minded competitive workforce capable of making a valuable contribution to the economic development of their own countries while enabling their higher education institutions to enter the international rankings of the world’s top universities.
